steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents two distinct benefits of community science: it promotes conservation engagement and it greatly expands data collection. The correct answer must capture both benefits accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Maya Whitfield and colleagues used a community science approach in their butterfly research and were surprised at the effect this had on conservation efforts.’: Focuses on a single researcher's reaction to one study rather than summarizing the passage's overall claim about community science. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘A community science approach can benefit communities by increasing conservation efforts, but it's less likely to help researchers obtain accurate data.’: Directly contradicts the passage, which states that community science greatly increases data collection. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘A community science approach can increase conservation efforts and generate large amounts of data, benefiting both communities and researchers.’: Captures both benefits the passage identifies — community engagement through conservation and large-scale data generation for researchers. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘A community science approach allows researchers to collect significantly more data even though it's unlikely to have much of an effect on conservation efforts.’: Also contradicts the passage, which presents increased conservation efforts as a key benefit. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
